overall_structure.description:
A single, self-contained, highly ornamented twin‑tower suspension bridge inspired by London Tower Bridge. The composition features:
      - A 3‑wide, 11‑long deck spine running north–south (y axis), with layered edge planks to create depth and parapet lines.
      - Two Gothic‑styled towers positioned symmetrically at y = −3 and y = +3, each composed of paired outer buttress columns that rise above the deck, cross-caps, mid‑height transoms, and decorative “rose window” powered wheels on the tower faces. Cylindrical turret‑like water cannons crown the towers, and torches serve as finials/lanterns.
      - A light high‑level walkway centered at x = 0 and z ≈ 5 over the central span between the towers, evoking Tower Bridge’s upper walkways.
      - Suspension by rope elements (winches) from the inner faces of the tower tops down to the deck, forming elegant catenary‑like lines and clearly establishing a suspended deck.
      - Discrete cross‑bracing (braces) between buttresses for a lace‑like Gothic look, and a few walkway connection braces for visual and structural unity.
      The whole structure is one sub‑structure, balanced, symmetrical, and visually rich, employing every available block type for maximum variety and ornamentation.
overall_structure.functionality:
- Spanning: The deck is 11 units long, intentionally longer than the 10‑unit terrain gap, so when the bridge is placed above the gap and allowed to settle, its ends project onto both sides and rest on the flat terrains without any fixed connection.
      - Height: The deck plane is the main horizontal layer; towers rise well above it. When the bridge rests on the terrain edges, the deck remains 5+ units above the gap’s bottom (typical canyon depth), satisfying the deck‑height requirement.
      - Suspension: Four rope winches (two per tower) drop from high points on the towers to the deck top faces near midspan, making the deck clearly suspended from the towers rather than solely supported from below.
      - Stability and aesthetic detail: Buttress cross‑bracing and a minimal high‑level walkway add stiffness and the Tower Bridge signature. Wheels, cannons, and torches supply ornamental richness while also distributing mass evenly.
      - No active propulsion is needed; wheels remain unpowered and cannons unfired. The bridge remains static after placement.
overall_structure.assembly:
Single sub‑structure assembled as follows (coordinates given relative to the default 1×1×1 stone base at the geometric center; positive y is north, positive x is east, positive z is up):
      1) Deck core (x = 0 centerline):
         - From the stone base, build a central spine of 11 Small Wooden Blocks along y from −5 to +5 at z = 0 (one block per unit). Start by attaching to both the +y and −y faces of the base, then extend outward to complete the line.
         - At each of the 11 centerline blocks, attach two lateral planks: one to the +x face (x = +1) and one to the −x face (x = −1), both at z = 0. This yields a symmetric 3‑wide deck (x = −1, 0, +1) along y ∈ [−5, +5].
      2) Tower frames (at y = −3 and y = +3, symmetrical):
         - Buttress columns (per tower): From the deck’s outer planks at that y (x = −1 and x = +1), attach a Small Wooden Block to the outer side faces to start the buttresses at x = −2 and x = +2, z = 0. Stack 5 more blocks straight up on each to reach z = 5 (6‑high buttresses per side).
         - Mid‑height transom (z = 3): From the left buttress (x = −2, z = 3), extend a 2‑block horizontal run toward the center across x = −1 then x = 0. From the right buttress (x = +2, z = 3), add 1 block toward x = +1 for symmetry and layering.
         - Top cross‑cap (z = 5): From the left buttress (x = −2), add 2 blocks across x to x = −1 and x = 0. From the right buttress (x = +2), add 1 block across x to x = +1. This creates a stepped cap with a center top block at x = 0 and a slightly stepped form on the right, enhancing the Gothic, layered silhouette.
         - Rose windows: Attach a Powered Wheel to the outward vertical face of the transom’s center block (x = 0, z = 3) on each tower—use the south face on the south tower (y = −3) and the north face on the north tower (y = +3). The wheel discs act as large circular windows. Do not activate them.
         - Turrets and lanterns: Attach a Water Cannon to the top face of the center top cross‑cap block (x = 0, z = 5) on each tower, oriented upward to serve as a turret finial. On the outer vertical faces of the topmost buttress blocks (x = ±2, z = 5), mount a Torch pointing upward (two per tower). Space is ample to avoid heating the cannons (keep >0.3 units from any cannon surface).
      3) High‑level walkway (x = 0, z ≈ 5):
         - From the deck center block at y = 0 (x = 0, z = 0), build a 5‑block vertical riser straight up to z = 5.
         - From the riser top (x = 0, z = 5), extend a 5‑block run along y from −2 to +2 (skip placing blocks at y = ±3 to avoid overlap with the tower cross‑caps). Use two braces (see below) to connect each walkway end (y = −2 and y = +2) to the nearest tower top cross‑cap center blocks at y = −3 and y = +3 respectively to visually complete the high‑level walkway span.
      4) Suspension ropes (winches):
         - South tower (y = −3):
           • Winch A: Connect from the inner face of the left buttress top block (x = −2, z = 5) to the top face of the deck centerline block at y = −2 (x = 0, z = 0).
           • Winch B: Connect from the inner face of the right buttress top block (x = +2, z = 5) to the top face of the deck centerline block at y = −1 (x = 0, z = 0).
         - North tower (y = +3):
           • Winch C: Connect from the inner face of the left buttress top block (x = −2, z = 5) to the top face of the deck centerline block at y = +1 (x = 0, z = 0).
           • Winch D: Connect from the inner face of the right buttress top block (x = +2, z = 5) to the top face of the deck centerline block at y = +2 (x = 0, z = 0).
         These four ropes create a clear suspended deck visual and functional scheme.
      5) Bracing and ornament:
         - Tower cross‑bracing (per tower): Install two Braces in X‑patterns between the inner faces of the buttresses—one from left (z = 1) to right (z = 3), and one from left (z = 3) to right (z = 1). Repeat on both towers.
         - Walkway tie‑ins: Use one Brace from the walkway end at y = −2 (x = 0, z = 5) to the south tower center top block (x = 0, y = −3, z = 5), and one from the walkway end at y = +2 to the north tower center top block. These complete the high‑level walkway’s connection without additional blocks.
      6) Temporary scaffolds (for build staging only; remove before finalization):
         - While building the mid‑height transom and top cross‑cap, use temporary Small Wooden Blocks stacked between buttresses at z = 1–4 as internal supports for positioning. After placing the permanent members and attaching the braces and wheels, remove the temporary scaffolds to leave open, hollow tower interiors and archways that enhance visual richness.
      Overall bounding box (final): Length ≈ 11 (y: −5 to +5), width up to ≈ 4 (x: −2 to +2 including buttresses), height ≈ 7.5 (z: deck at 0, buttresses to 5, water cannons extend ≈ +1.75).
overall_structure.motion_control:
- Powered Wheels: Do not engage rotation during placement; leave unbound or off. They serve as static rose windows.
      - Water Cannons: Do not fire. Keep them unheated; torches are positioned >0.3 units away to avoid inadvertently switching to steam mode.
      - Winches (Ropes): Set to fixed lengths matching their spans (approximate line-of-sight lengths of 5.4–5.8 units). No reeling is required. They are passive suspension lines.
      - Expected motion during placement: None. The bridge, as a single sub‑structure, is placed above the gap and allowed to fall gently so its 11‑unit deck overhang rests on both terrain edges. No active stabilization is needed.

sub_structure_1.name: Twin‑Tower Suspended Deck Bridge (Single Piece)
sub_structure_1.description: One cohesive bridge inspired by London Tower Bridge: a central 3‑wide, 11‑long deck; two elevated towers with paired buttresses, rose‑window wheels, turret‑like cannons, and torch finials; a minimal high‑level walkway; ropes suspending the deck from the towers; and cross‑bracing that yields a lace‑like Gothic appearance. The result is symmetrical, vertically layered, and visually intricate.
sub_structure_1.components:
- Default 1×1×1 stone base (provided, central anchor)
        - Small Wooden Block: ~79 (deck spine and planks; tower buttresses, transoms, cross‑caps; riser and high‑level walkway segments)
        - Powered Wheel: 2 (rose windows on tower faces)
        - Water Cannon: 2 (turret finials on tower tops, unfired)
        - Torch: 4 (lantern/finial elements at tower tops, oriented upward, safely spaced)
        - Winch: 4 (primary suspension ropes)
        - Brace: 6 (4 tower cross‑braces, 2 walkway end tie‑ins)
sub_structure_1.assembly:
- Start from the base stone; create the 11‑block center spine along y at z = 0, then add left/right lateral planks at each spine block to achieve 3‑wide deck.
        - Build towers at y = −3 and +3: outward buttresses at x = ±2 (6 blocks tall), mid‑height transom toward center (to x = 0), and top cross‑cap (to x = 0). Place Powered Wheels on transom faces and Water Cannons on top cross‑cap center blocks (upward). Mount Torches on outer top buttress faces (upward).
        - Add the central riser (x = 0, y = 0, z = 0→5) and a 5‑block high‑level walkway run at z = 5 from y = −2 to +2. Tie its ends to the tower tops with Braces.
        - Install four Winches as suspension lines from inner faces of tower tops (x = ±2, z = 5) down to the top faces of deck centerline blocks at y = ±1 and ±2 per the pattern in the Overall Structure assembly.
        - Add two X‑braces between buttresses on each tower at the low and mid levels to complete the latticework.
        - Use, then remove, temporary wooden scaffolds within towers to stage transom and cross‑cap placements, leaving hollow interiors and archways.
sub_structure_1.motion_control:
- Keep wheels off (no key bound).
        - Do not fire water cannons. Maintain separation from torches; no steam mode.
        - Winches remain passive with fixed length; no reeling commands.
        - Placement: Lower the single sub‑structure so it gently seats on both terrain edges; no further actuation.
sub_structure_1.function:
- Spans the 10‑unit north–south gap with an 11‑unit deck, resting on both sides.
        - Provides two distinct towers rising above the deck with clear Tower Bridge styling.
        - Suspends the deck from the towers using rope (winch) elements.
        - Ensures the deck plane resides at least 5 units above the canyon bottom when seated on edges (typical terrain configuration), while presenting layered, hollow, and richly ornamented architecture using all available block types.
sub_structure_1.design_requirements:
- Total sub‑structure uses fewer than 100 elements even if connectors are counted; target bill as listed (approx. 79 wooden blocks + 8 detail blocks + 10 connectors = 97).
        - No overlapping placements; respect 1×1×1 block occupancy and the given attachment rules.
        - Keep torches ≥0.3 units away from any Water Cannon surface; orient torches upward. Do not fire cannons.
        - Ensure the 11‑unit deck length overhangs both terrain edges of the 10‑unit gap so the bridge self‑seats without anchors.
        - Ropes (winches) must connect between actual block faces (tower top inner faces to deck top faces) to represent suspension.
        - Temporary scaffolds are allowed during build but must be removed for the final hollow/tiered effect.
        - Positions may be micro‑adjusted in later stages to resolve conflicts based on actual build execution.
